name: Finance Expense Approver
description: Automates expense report submissions, validates receipts against company policy, routes for approval, and answers questions about the expense policy.
overview:
The Finance Expense Approver streamlines the entire expense management workflow. Employees submit expenses through a conversational interface — the agent validates each line item against company policy, flags violations, calculates totals with per diem rates, and routes the report for manager approval.

It handles the back-and-forth that typically bogs down finance teams: 'Is this expense category right?', 'Do I need a receipt for this amount?', 'What's the per diem for this city?', 'When will I get reimbursed?'

The agent connects to SAP Concur for expense processing and SharePoint for policy documents. Finance teams report 50% fewer policy-violation resubmissions and 3x faster approval cycles.
instructions:
You are a Finance Expense Approver assistant. Help employees submit expense reports correctly and answer expense policy questions.

Rules:
1. Always check expenses against the current company policy before approving
2. Receipts are required for any expense over $25
3. Flag expenses that exceed category limits but don't reject them — route to manager
4. Use current per diem rates from the GSA table for travel expenses
5. Meals with clients require attendee names and business purpose
6. Never approve expenses missing required documentation — return with clear instructions
7. Be helpful and non-judgmental — people make honest mistakes with expenses
8. Process within 24 hours SLA for standard reports, 4 hours for urgent/travel
author: evpeaire

topics.name: Submit Expense Report
topics.description: Guides employees through submitting a new expense report
topics.yamlContent:
kind: AdaptiveDialog
beginDialog:
  kind: OnRecognizedIntent
  id: submitExpense
  intent:
    triggerQueries:
      - Submit an expense report
      - I have expenses to submit
      - New expense report
  actions:
    - kind: Question
      id: expenseType
      prompt: What type of expense is this?
      variable: Topic.expenseType
      choices:
        - Travel (flights, hotel, transport)
        - Meals & Entertainment
        - Office Supplies
        - Software & Subscriptions
        - Training & Conferences
        - Other
    - kind: Question
      id: amount
      prompt: What's the total amount?
      variable: Topic.amount
    - kind: Question
      id: date
      prompt: When was this expense incurred?
      variable: Topic.expenseDate
    - kind: Question
      id: description
      prompt: Brief description and business purpose?
      variable: Topic.description
    - kind: InvokeAction
      id: validateExpense
      actionName: ValidateAndSubmitExpense

topics.name: Expense Policy Questions
topics.description: Answers questions about what's covered, limits, and requirements
topics.yamlContent:
kind: AdaptiveDialog
beginDialog:
  kind: OnRecognizedIntent
  id: policyQuestion
  intent:
    triggerQueries:
      - What's the expense policy
      - Can I expense this
      - What's the limit for
  actions:
    - kind: SendActivity
      id: policyOverview
      activity:
        text: |
          Here are the key expense policy highlights:
          
          **Receipts:** Required for any expense over $25
          **Meals:** $75/day domestic, $100/day international
          **Hotels:** Follow GSA per diem rates for the city
          **Flights:** Economy class for domestic, premium economy for 6+ hours
          **Software:** Up to $50/month without approval, above needs manager sign-off
          
          What specific expense are you wondering about?

topics.name: Check Reimbursement Status
topics.description: Looks up the status of a submitted expense report
topics.yamlContent:
kind: AdaptiveDialog
beginDialog:
  kind: OnRecognizedIntent
  id: checkStatus
  intent:
    triggerQueries:
      - Where's my reimbursement
      - Expense report status
      - When will I get paid back
  actions:
    - kind: Question
      id: reportId
      prompt: Do you have the expense report number? If not, I can look it up by date.
      variable: Topic.reportId
    - kind: InvokeAction
      id: lookupStatus
      actionName: CheckExpenseStatus
      input:
        reportId: =Topic.reportId

tools.name: SAP Concur API
tools.description: Submits expense reports, checks approval status, and queries reimbursement timelines

tools.name: SharePoint Policy Library
tools.description: Retrieves current expense policy documents, per diem tables, and category limits

tools.name: Excel GSA Per Diem Rates
tools.description: Looks up current government per diem rates by city for travel expense validation

powerAutomateFlows.name: Validate and Submit Expense
powerAutomateFlows.description: Validates expense against policy limits, checks for receipt attachment, and submits to Concur with proper categorization
powerAutomateFlows.purpose: Catches policy violations before submission, reducing rejection rates

powerAutomateFlows.name: Check Expense Status
powerAutomateFlows.description: Queries Concur for the current status of an expense report — pending, approved, processing, or paid
powerAutomateFlows.purpose: Gives employees instant visibility into their reimbursement status

powerAutomateFlows.name: Manager Approval Reminder
powerAutomateFlows.description: Sends a Teams reminder to managers with expense reports pending approval for more than 48 hours
powerAutomateFlows.purpose: Keeps approval cycles short and employees happy

additionalSections.title: Prerequisites
additionalSections.content:
1. SAP Concur instance with API credentials
2. SharePoint site with expense policy documents
3. Excel workbook with current GSA per diem rates (updated annually)
4. Teams channel for finance team notifications

additionalSections.title: Configuration Notes
additionalSections.content:
- Update per diem rates in the Excel workbook each October when GSA publishes new rates
- Category spending limits are defined in the SharePoint policy doc — update there to change limits
- The 48-hour approval reminder runs daily at 9 AM UTC
- Receipt OCR validation requires AI Builder credits

additionalSections.title: Deployment Notes
additionalSections.content:
After importing the solution:
1. Update the SAP Concur custom connector with your instance URL and API key
2. Set the SharePoint site URL in the policy lookup flow
3. Configure the per diem Excel file location
4. Test with a $0 expense report to verify end-to-end flow
